concept_name: Magnetic Field of a Long Straight Current-Carrying Wire (Right-Hand Rule)
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism §4.4 (Magnetic field due to a current element / long straight wire) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. The atomic concept: the DIRECTION (right-hand rule, azimuthal, tangent) and MAGNITUDE (B = μ₀I/2πr) of B around a long straight current-carrying wire. Restructured 2026-07 to the Rule 31 straightforward + per-state-contextual-controls model, mirroring the just-approved, baseline-locked magnetic_field_concept_B — shares the straight_wire_current field_3d scenario and the swc (per-state control-panel) pattern concept_B seeded. This is the SECOND atom in the Ch.4 straight-wire pair: concept_B teaches WHAT B is (a vector field sourced by moving charge, azimuthal, revealed by a compass) with NO magnitude; THIS concept owns the RIGHT-HAND-RULE cases (I up → CCW, I down → CW), the TANGENT-AT-A-POINT idea, the ×/· 2D bridge, and the MAGNITUDE payoff B = μ₀I/(2πr) — STATE_7's sandbox is the only state that ever shows the numeric readout. EPIC-L-first, conceptual-only (no epic_c_branches, no mode_overrides — Rule 20 suspension). Most-confused Class 12 magnetism concept: students think B points along I, conflate ×/· with current direction, try the right-hand rule on flat paper, and assume reversing current changes B's magnitude.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture a USB cable plugged into a power bank charging your phone. A small steady current flows in the wire. Hold a magnetic compass within a few centimeters of the wire. Watch the needle swing — it does NOT point to magnetic north anymore; it tilts perpendicular to the wire. Switch off the current — the needle snaps back. The wire creates a magnetic field that wraps around it in 3D circles, strong enough to overpower Earth's field at close range.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture an overhead high-tension power line above a Delhi street. Hundreds of amperes flow through it. Standing directly below with a sensitive compass, you would see the needle deflect perceptibly even from several meters away — because B falls off only as 1/r, not 1/r², and the current is enormous.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture an MRI machine's main coil — a current-carrying tube with the patient inside. The tube creates a magnetic field along its axis (this is actually a solenoid, but each turn is a loop, and each tiny segment of each loop is a tiny straight current-carrying piece). The whole field of the machine is just countless straight-wire B-fields added up. Master the single straight wire, you understand every magnet in the world.

r.name: Perpendicular distance from wire to field point

formulas.direction: B is tangent to the circle of radius r centered on the wire, in the plane perpendicular to the wire at the field point
formulas.right_hand_rule: Point right thumb along current I; curled fingers give the sense (clockwise/counter-clockwise) of B around the wire

constraints: Wire is treated as infinitely long and infinitesimally thin (idealization holds when r << wire length and r >> wire diameter)
B-field at any point depends ONLY on the perpendicular distance r from the wire — not on position along the wire
B is everywhere perpendicular to the radial direction (tangent to the circle through the point) — zero radial component, zero axial component, purely azimuthal

I is authored as an unsigned magnitude (A); the S3→S4 current reversal is encoded via current_direction_indicator/field_rotation_direction, never by negating I — magnitude stays constant across both cases
Direction (CCW/CW sense) reverses if current direction reverses; magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr) never changes with direction
hide_magnitude_readout:true + show_readout:false on STATES 1-6; false/true ONLY on STATE_7 — the magnitude payoff this concept owns is deliberately withheld until the explore state

aha_moment.statement: Point your right thumb along the current; your curling fingers ARE the direction of B.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The right-hand curl visibly matches the ring rotation in STATE_3; reversing to STATE_4 flips both the hand and the rings together.

STATE_1.title: Oersted's Hook — a Current Deflects a Compass

scene_composition.text: Wire + battery — switch open, no current yet
compass needle — watch it swing
1820: Oersted's discovery — current makes a magnetic field

tts_sentences.text_en: Here's a wire beside a compass. Switch open, no current — the needle just points north.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक wire है, compass के बगल में। Switch open है, कोई current नहीं — needle बस north की तरफ इशारा करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ ఒక wire ఉంది, compass పక్కన. Switch open, current లేదు — needle కేవలం north వైపు చూపిస్తోంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The switch closes — current flows up the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Switch close होता है — wire में ऊपर की तरफ current बहने लगती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Switch close అవుతుంది — wire లో పైకి current ప్రవహించడం మొదలవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the needle swing away from north, moved by nothing but that current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए needle north से हट जाती है — इसे और किसी चीज़ ने नहीं, बस उस current ने हिलाया।
tts_sentences.text_te: needle north నుండి తొలగిపోవడం చూడండి — దీన్ని కదిలించింది ఆ current తప్ప మరేమీ కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: No magnet is here — moving charge alone made this field, just as Oersted found in 1820.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ कोई magnet नहीं है — सिर्फ moving charge ने यह field बनाई, जैसा Oersted ने 1820 में पाया।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ magnet ఏదీ లేదు — కేవలం moving charge ఈ field ను సృష్టించింది, 1820 లో Oersted కనుగొన్నట్టుగానే.

misconception_watch.belief: Electricity and magnetism are unrelated — a wire is just a wire
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The needle only deflects while current actually flows, and snaps back the instant the switch opens.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A current-carrying wire IS a source of magnetic field — no current, no field.

STATE_2.title: B Is 3D Circles — Never Along the Wire

scene_composition.text: Magnetic field — full 3D, current flowing
NOT along the wire, NOT straight out
Circles loop around the wire at every height

tts_sentences.text_en: With current flowing, the wire fills the space around it with a magnetic field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current बहने के साथ, wire अपने चारों ओर की space को magnetic field से भर देती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: current ప్రవహిస్తున్నప్పుడు, wire తన చుట్టూ ఉన్న space ని magnetic field తో నింపుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: It doesn't point along the wire, and it doesn't point straight out either.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह wire के along point नहीं करती, और सीधे बाहर की तरफ भी नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది wire వెంట point చేయదు, నేరుగా బయటకు కూడా చేయదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch — the field joins into full 3D circles that loop around the wire, not flat rings on paper.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए — field पूरे 3D circles में जुड़ जाती है जो wire के चारों ओर loop करती हैं, कागज़ पर चपटे rings नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి — field పూర్తి 3D circles గా చేరుతుంది, ఇవి wire చుట్టూ loop అవుతాయి, కాగితం మీద flat rings కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: The magnetic field points in the same direction as the current — it flows along the wire
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The ghost arrow drawn along the wire fades out as the real tangent rings assemble and rotate around it, never along it.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: B circles around the wire, perpendicular to it — never along it.

STATE_3.title: Case A — Current UP, the Right-Hand Curl Gives B

scene_composition.text: Case A — Current UP
Right thumb along I — fingers curl to B
B circles run counter-clockwise (from above)

tts_sentences.text_en: Current flows up this wire — Case A.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इस wire में current ऊपर की तरफ बहती है — Case A।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ wire లో current పైకి ప్రవహిస్తుంది — Case A.

tts_sentences.text_en: Point your right thumb along the current — your curling fingers are the direction of magnetic field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अपना right thumb current के along रखिए — आपकी curling fingers ही magnetic field B की direction हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ right thumb ని current వెంట పెట్టండి — మీ curling fingers ఏవైతే ఉన్నాయో అవే magnetic field B యొక్క direction.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the rings turn counter-clockwise, exactly matching your fingers' curl.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए rings counter-clockwise घूमती हैं — बिल्कुल आपकी fingers के curl जैसी।
tts_sentences.text_te: rings counter-clockwise గా తిరగడం చూడండి — సరిగ్గా మీ fingers యొక్క curl లాగే.

tts_sentences.text_en: Always the right hand, never the left — hold this picture, we flip the current next.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हमेशा right hand, कभी left नहीं — इसे याद रखिए, अब हम current flip करेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎప్పుడూ right hand, ఎప్పటికీ left కాదు — దీన్ని గుర్తుంచుకోండి, ఇప్పుడు మనం current ని flip చేస్తాం.

misconception_watch.belief: Use the left hand — it's the natural one to wrap around a vertical wire
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Only a right hand ever appears, curling in the same sense the rings rotate.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Always the RIGHT hand for B around a current — never the left.

STATE_4.title: Case B — Current DOWN, the Curl Flips Too

scene_composition.text: Case B — Current DOWN
Right thumb down — fingers curl to B
B circles run clockwise (from above)

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the current flows DOWN the wire — the opposite direction from Case A.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब current wire में नीचे की तरफ बहती है — Case A से उल्टी direction।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు current wire లో కిందికి ప్రవహిస్తుంది — Case A కి వ్యతిరేక direction లో.

tts_sentences.text_en: Point your right thumb down this time — your fingers curl clockwise.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इस बार अपना right thumb नीचे रखिए — आपकी fingers clockwise curl होती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈసారి మీ right thumb ని కిందికి పెట్టండి — మీ fingers clockwise గా curl అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The rings turn clockwise, matching your hand — same right hand, opposite thumb.
tts_sentences.text_hi: rings clockwise घूमती हैं, आपके हाथ जैसी — same right hand, opposite thumb।
tts_sentences.text_te: rings clockwise గా తిరుగుతాయి, మీ చేతిలాగే — same right hand, opposite thumb.

tts_sentences.text_en: Only the direction flipped from Case A — the strength B = μ₀I/2πr never changed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: सिर्फ Case A से direction बदली — strength B = μ₀I/2πr कभी नहीं बदलती।
tts_sentences.text_te: కేవలం Case A నుండి direction మారింది — strength B = μ₀I/2πr ఎప్పుడూ మారదు.

misconception_watch.belief: The magnetic field's strength depends on which way the current flows
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The rings keep the same brightness and count as Case A — only their rotation sense flips.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr) is identical for both directions — only the direction of B flips.

STATE_5.title: B at a Single Point — Tangent, Never Radial

scene_composition.text: Pick point P — which way does B point?
B at P is TANGENT to the circle
Never radial, never along the wire

tts_sentences.text_en: Pick one point P beside the wire — which way does B point exactly there?
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire के पास एक point P चुनिए — वहाँ ठीक B किस तरफ point करती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: wire పక్కన ఒక point P తీసుకోండి — సరిగ్గా అక్కడ B ఏ దిశలో point చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: It's tangent to the circle through P — never radial, never along the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह P से गुज़रने वाले circle पर tangent है — radial नहीं, wire के along भी नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది P గుండా వెళ్ళే circle కి tangent — radial కాదు, wire వెంట కూడా కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your right-hand curl gives you this exact tangent direction at any point you choose.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपका right-hand curl किसी भी point पर यही exact tangent direction देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ right-hand curl ఏ point వద్దనైనా ఇదే exact tangent direction ఇస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The field radiates straight outward from the wire, like spokes from a hub
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The tangent arrow at P sits perpendicular to the wire-to-P line, never along that line.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: B at a point is tangent to the circle through it — never radial.

STATE_6.title: Bridge to 2D — Where × and · Come From

scene_composition.text: Looking down the wire — the exam-diagram angle
× = field INTO the page
· = field OUT of the page

tts_sentences.text_en: Now look straight down the wire — the angle exam diagrams use.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब wire को सीधे ऊपर से देखिए — यही angle exam diagrams use करती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు wire ని నేరుగా పైనుండి చూడండి — ఇదే angle exam diagrams వాడతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Exam diagrams flatten this into two symbols — a cross for field INTO the page.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Exam diagrams इसे दो symbols में flatten करती हैं — cross जहाँ field page के अंदर जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Exam diagrams దీన్ని రెండు symbols గా flatten చేస్తాయి — cross ఎక్కడైతే field page లోపలికి వెళుతుందో అక్కడ.

tts_sentences.text_en: A dot marks field coming back OUT — same field, two sides.
tts_sentences.text_hi: dot दिखाती है जहाँ field वापस बाहर आती है — same field, दो तरफ से।
tts_sentences.text_te: field మళ్ళీ బయటకు వచ్చే చోట dot చూపిస్తుంది — same field, రెండు వైపుల నుండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same wire, same current — both symbols label the FIELD, never the current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same wire, same current — दोनों symbols FIELD को label करते हैं, कभी current को नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Same wire, same current — రెండు symbols FIELD ని label చేస్తాయి, ఎప్పుడూ current ని కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: The × and · symbols show which way the current flows
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Both symbols describe the SAME single wire carrying ONE current — they can't be marking two different currents.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: × and · label the magnetic FIELD direction at a point, never the current.

STATE_7.title: Explore — Drag I and r, Watch B Respond Live

scene_composition.text: Drag the current and distance sliders
Move closer or crank up current — B responds live
Same wire, same rule — B = μ₀I / 2πr

tts_sentences.text_en: Now it's your turn — drag the current and distance sliders and watch magnetic field B change live.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी — current और distance sliders drag कीजिए और magnetic field B को live बदलते देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు — current మరియు distance sliders ని drag చేయండి మరియు magnetic field B live గా మారడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Move closer or crank up the current — see how quickly magnetic field B responds.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पास आइए या current बढ़ाइए — देखिए magnetic field B कितनी जल्दी respond करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దగ్గరగా రండి లేదా current పెంచండి — magnetic field B ఎంత త్వరగా respond అవుతుందో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same wire, same right-hand rule — the numbers just scale with B = μ₀I over 2πr.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same wire, same right-hand rule — numbers बस B = μ₀I / 2πr के साथ scale होते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Same wire, same right-hand rule — numbers కేవలం B = μ₀I / 2πr తో scale అవుతాయి.

STATE_1.label: Oersted's hook — switch closes, needle swings

STATE_2.label: B is 3D circles — never along the wire

STATE_3.label: Case A — I up, right-hand curl gives B counter-clockwise

STATE_4.label: Case B — I down, right-hand curl gives B clockwise

STATE_5.label: B at point P — tangent to the circle, never radial

STATE_6.label: Top-down bridge — × into page, · out of page

STATE_7.label: Free explore — sliders + rotate
STATE_7.formula_overlay:
Right-hand rule:
thumb ↑ I,
fingers ↑ B

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered this concept when they can state that a current-carrying wire is the SOURCE of a magnetic field (Oersted) — current on, field on; current off, field gone; that the right-hand rule (never the left hand) gives the sense of B around the wire — thumb along I, fingers curl in the direction of B — counter-clockwise for current up, clockwise for current down (viewed from above); that reversing the current reverses ONLY the direction of B, never its magnitude; that at any single point B is TANGENT to the circle through that point, perpendicular to the wire, never radial and never along the wire; that the × and · symbols in 2D textbook diagrams denote the FIELD direction at a point (into/out of the page), never the current direction, and can both appear on the same single wire; and that the magnitude follows B = μ₀I/(2πr) — directly proportional to current, inversely proportional to distance.

questions.stem: With the switch open, a compass near the wire points north. You close the switch and the needle swings away from north; you open it again and the needle returns to north. What does this experiment show?

options.A: The needle only responded to the switch clicking — a mechanical vibration, not anything magnetic
options.B: A current in the wire produces a magnetic field — switching the current off removes the field
options.C: The wire itself is a hidden permanent magnet; the current has nothing to do with it
options.D: Compass needles naturally drift back and forth on their own; the timing was a coincidence

questions.tested_idea: A current-carrying wire is the source of a magnetic field — current and magnetism are directly causally linked (Oersted).
questions.parallel_form_stem: A phone charger's wire is live; a compass beside it deflects. Unplug it and the needle returns to north. What caused the deflection?

questions.stem: A long straight wire carries current I straight UP. Using the right-hand rule, which way do the magnetic field circles run, viewed from above?

options.A: Counter-clockwise — point the right thumb up along I, fingers curl counter-clockwise
options.B: Clockwise — point the LEFT thumb up along I, fingers curl clockwise
options.C: Clockwise — the right thumb along I gives clockwise curl for upward current
options.D: There is no fixed sense — the field circles randomly around the wire

questions.tested_idea: The right-hand rule (never left) gives the CCW sense of B for upward current: thumb along I, fingers curl in the direction of B.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Current flows upward through a vertical wire. Point your right thumb along the current — which way do your fingers curl, viewed from above?

questions.stem: You reverse the battery so the SAME wire now carries the SAME magnitude of current, but flowing DOWN instead of up. What changes about the magnetic field?

options.A: B becomes weaker, because reversing the current direction reduces its strength
options.B: B disappears entirely — reversing the current cancels the magnetic field
options.C: Only the direction of B reverses (now clockwise from above); the magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr) is unchanged
options.D: Nothing changes at all — the direction of current does not affect B in any way

questions.tested_idea: Reversing current direction reverses the SENSE of B (CCW→CW) but leaves the MAGNITUDE B = μ₀I/(2πr) completely unchanged.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A wire's current is reversed but kept at the same amperage. Compare the new magnetic field to the old one — what stayed the same, what changed?

questions.stem: At a single point P beside a current-carrying wire, which way does the magnetic field B actually point?

options.A: Straight out from the wire toward P, along the line joining wire and P (radially)
options.B: Tangent to the circle of radius r through P — perpendicular to the line joining the wire to P, never along that line
options.C: Straight along the wire itself, parallel to the current
options.D: Straight toward the wire, pulling P inward like gravity

questions.tested_idea: B at a point is TANGENT to the circle through that point — never radial, never along the wire.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Pick a point 5 cm to the side of a vertical current-carrying wire. In which direction does the field point exactly at that point?

questions.stem: A textbook diagram shows a single wire carrying ONE current, but draws a × on one side and a · on the other side of the wire's cross-section. What do × and · actually represent?

options.A: The magnetic field direction at each point — × means B into the page, · means B out of the page
options.B: The current direction — × means current goes into the page there, · means it comes out
options.C: Two different currents flowing in the same wire, one in each direction
options.D: The polarity of the wire — × is the negative end, · is the positive end

questions.tested_idea: × and · label the magnetic FIELD direction at that point (into/out of the page), never the current — a single wire, single current, can show both.
questions.parallel_form_stem: In a diagram, one side of a current-carrying wire is marked ⊗ and the other ⊙. Since it is the same one wire, what must these two symbols be describing?

questions.stem: Using B = μ₀I/(2πr), if you DOUBLE the current I and also DOUBLE the distance r from the wire, what happens to the magnetic field B?

options.A: B doubles — since both I and r doubled, effects add and B ends up twice as strong
options.B: B halves — increasing distance always weakens a field, regardless of the current
options.C: B stays exactly the same — doubling I doubles B, but doubling r halves B, and the two cancel
options.D: B quadruples — because B depends on I² according to the formula

questions.tested_idea: B = μ₀I/(2πr): B is directly proportional to I and inversely proportional to r — the two effects can cancel exactly.
questions.parallel_form_stem: At distance r from a wire carrying current I, B = 20 µT. If you move to distance 2r and also increase the current to 2I, what is the new B?
